threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stop at the traffic lights on Dalston Junction, waiting for a green man. Locals just cross wherever, like it’s a game of hopscotch.
Caught a glimpse of a couple on the 59 sharing a kebab like it was a romantic feast. Only in Brixton could a night bus feel so special.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last summer, I watched a guy juggle flaming torches outside the Reuterplatz while kids danced around him. That's when it hit me: this place is magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a beer after midnight at a Späti while debating the best Döner kebab joint with a stranger. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals know to dodge the BART Civic Center station after the latest loud bang. Tourists still standing around confused, phones out.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 one evening, packed with locals and tourists alike, all jamming to a street musician. Only in SF do you get a full concert on your way home.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into any bar for tapas at 8 PM. Real locals know that's when the kitchen closes, and we're just getting started.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their over-reliance on Google Maps for directions. Locals navigate by landmarks, not just the shortest route.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus is a reliable option. Locals know it’s hit or miss at best, especially on weekends.
Caught a sight of a bloke arguing with a lamppost at 3 AM on the bus to Streatham. Must’ve lost a bet or something; the lamppost didn’t budge.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy casually carrying a whole goat down the street for a BBQ in Tempelhof. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find open-air karaoke in Görlitzer Park every summer. Just random strangers belting out 80s hits on a Wednesday night. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a true local if they instinctively avoid the Civic Center BART station after hearing one loud bang—better to walk than risk the chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a Muni driver jump out to chase a runaway dog down Larkin. Took a solid five minutes but the pup made it back safe. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the sangria and tapas. Hardly anyone drinks that here; it's vermut and pa amb tomàquet if you want the real deal.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they hesitate at crosswalks, waiting for the green man, even when there’s no traffic.
